quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Ensure consistent brand hierarchy and visual balance across layouts.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide where blues and golds dominate, and tertiary colors are used as accents and surfaces.

quotes: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds)
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain readability and contrast of critical headings.
rules.rule_text: Headlines and headings must use Brand Blue (color.primary.brand_blue = #00529b), or White (color.tertiary.white = #ffffff) on dark/gradient backgrounds.

quotes: Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure optimal legibility and standard contrast for body copy.
rules.rule_text: Body text must use Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds, and White (color.tertiary.white = #ffffff) on dark backgrounds, with increased line height on dark backgrounds.

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Maintain programmatic semantic use of color within clinical visualizations.
rules.rule_text: Charts must use Brand Blue (color.primary.brand_blue = #00529b) and Sky Blue (color.secondary.sky_blue = #358CCB) as dominant colors, with Sunshine (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b) or Gold (color.primary.gold = #ffc60a) strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures, and gray for placebo.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts)
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Provide distinct, readable background colors for different styles of callouts.
rules.rule_text: For callout surfaces, data callouts use Light Blue (color.tertiary.light_blue = #E6F0F9) at 70% tint (opacity.logo.gold = 0.7), and text-heavy callouts use Golden Sand (color.tertiary.golden_sand = #FEF1C8) at 30% tint (opacity.callout.golden_sand_box = 0.3).

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL]: avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent misuse of an energetic accent color on functional interface or editorial text elements.
rules.rule_text: Coral (color.tertiary.coral = #F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. Avoid Coral in email sections unless explicitly required by the brief.

quotes: underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Maintain legal and UX clarity for interactive elements within flow text.
rules.rule_text: Links must be underlined and use Brand Blue (color.primary.brand_blue = #00529b) on light backgrounds, and White (color.tertiary.white = #ffffff) on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Protect the visual integrity of trademark/brand identity marks.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / Gold (logo.mark.opacity = 0.7) must be maintained in all instances, including both the logo itself and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic.
